message:
fix(mutex): prevent spurious wakeups and double-registration in async Mutex

The async Mutex implementation had a subtle bug where a waiter could be
woken spuriously (before actually acquiring the lock) and then re-register
itself in the waiter queue, leading to duplicate entries. This caused
inefficient wake cascades and potential fairness issues.

Key changes:
- Replace simple `Waker` in waiter queue with `Waiter` struct that tracks:
  - `waker`: The actual Waker to call
  - `queued`: Arc<AtomicBool> indicating if this entry is still valid
- On unlock, skip waiters where `queued` is already false (already woken)
- When a waiter is woken but doesn't get the lock, it re-registers with
  the same `queued` flag, preventing duplicate queue entries
- On lock acquisition, mark the waiter as no longer queued

This follows the intrusive linked list pattern commonly used in async
runtimes, providing proper deregistration semantics without the overhead
of actual intrusive data structures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-39ik","title":"HTTP Server/Client Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, protocol)","description":"# HTTP Server/Client Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the HTTP layer (if7, hyper equivalent) ensuring protocol compliance, cancel-correctness, and performance.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Request parsing: methods, headers, body
- Response construction: status, headers, body
- HTTP/1.1: keep-alive, chunked encoding, pipelining
- HTTP/2: streams, flow control, HPACK
- Connection management: pooling, reuse

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| GET request/response | Basic HTTP lifecycle |
| POST with body | Request body handling |
| Streaming response | Chunked transfer |
| Keep-alive reuse | Connection pooling |
| HTTP/2 multiplexing | Stream concurrency |
| Cancel mid-request | Client abort handling |
| Server shutdown | Graceful drain |
| Large body transfer | Memory efficiency |

### 3. Protocol Compliance Tests
- RFC 9110 (HTTP Semantics)
- RFC 9112 (HTTP/1.1)
- RFC 9113 (HTTP/2)
- Header validation
- Content-Length vs Transfer-Encoding

### 4. Performance Tests
- Requests per second baseline
- Concurrent connection handling
- Memory usage under load
- Latency distribution (p50, p95, p99)

### 5. Security Tests
- Request smuggling prevention
- Header injection prevention
- Timeout enforcement
- Resource exhaustion protection

## Logging Requirements
- HTTP events logged with request IDs
- On failure: dump connection state, pending requests
- Timing breakdown: parsing, processing, serialization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Protocol compliance tests pass
- [ ] E2E scenarios cover HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for client and server
- [ ] Performance baselines established
- [ ] `cargo test http` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib http
cargo test --test http_e2e
cargo bench --bench http_perf
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:38.086653259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:35.738379666Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:35.738315567Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39ik","depends_on_id":"asupersync-if7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Triage + prioritize fixes (focus on critical first).","notes":"Ran UBS on src/. Critical: std::thread::spawn without join in time/sleep.rs and transport/mock.rs. Warnings: TcpStream shutdown missing in net/* + runtime/reactor/source.rs.  {"id":"bd-obpv","title":"Time/Timers Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, determinism)","description":"# Time/Timers Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the time/timer infrastructure (mf6) ensuring virtual/wall time correctness, budget integration, and deterministic behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- sleep(duration): basic pause
- sleep_until(instant): absolute time pause
- interval(period): repeating timer
- interval_at(start, period): explicit start
- MissedTickBehavior: Burst, Delay, Skip
- Timer heap operations: insert, pop, cancel

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Sleep + cancel | Clean cancellation, Cancelled outcome |
| Nested timeouts | Budget propagation |
| Interval tick counting | Correct tick delivery |
| Missed tick burst | Catch-up behavior |
| Concurrent timers | No timer starvation |
| Budget exhaustion | Deadline enforcement |

### 3. Virtual vs Wall Time Tests
- Lab runtime: virtual time advances only on explicit call
- Production: wall time advances independently
- Cross-mode: same test logic, different time source

### 4. Determinism Tests
- Same seed → identical timer ordering
- Replay tests: record and replay timer sequences
- No wall-clock leakage in lab mode

## Logging Requirements
- Timer events logged with virtual/wall timestamps
- On failure: dump timer heap state, pending timers
- Deadline violations logged with budget context

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All timer operations have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass under both lab and production runtime
- [ ] Determinism verified via replay tests
- [ ] Budget integration tests pass
- [ ] `cargo test time` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib time
cargo test --test time_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"FrostyCanyon","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:46:51.972964834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:05:51.881152384Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:05:51.881080620Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. 469 unit tests (sleep, interval, timeout, virtual_time_wheel, budget), 42 E2E tests (lab runtime, virtual time, determinism, budget integration), 9 production runtime tests. Determinism: determinism_across_runs, sleep_deterministic_completion, interval_deterministic_sequence. Budget: budget_deadline_propagation, time_from_budget_deadline. All 520 tests pass with 0 failures.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-obpv","depends_on_id":"asupersync-mf6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-q0om","title":"[Harmonize] Merge I/O EPICs: 90l9 → ds8","description":"# [Harmonize] Merge I/O EPICs: 90l9 → ds8

## Purpose
Migrate all unique sub-EPICs from asupersync-90l9 into asupersync-ds8 hierarchy, then close 90l9.

## Sub-EPICs to Migrate
- wx8h: Core Reactor
- l92b: epoll
- 3z8z: kqueue  
- 7tk3: Lab Reactor
- ui2r: TCP/UDP
- ycir: UDS
- 56fs: Integration Testing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 90l9 sub-EPICs linked to ds8 hierarchy via dependencies
- [ ] 90l9 closed with migration note referencing ds8
- [ ] No orphaned sub-EPICs after merge
- [ ] ds8 description updated to reflect absorbed scope
- [ ] `br show asupersync-ds8` shows correct dependent tree

## Verification
```bash
br show asupersync-90l9  # Should be closed
br show asupersync-ds8   # Should show all I/O sub-EPICs
bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'  # No cycles
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus45","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:16.599858279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:23:32.548968635Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:23:32.548896109Z","close_reason":"Verified: 90l9 already merged into ds8 hierarchy. All sub-EPICs (wx8h, l92b, 3z8z, 7tk3, ui2r, ycir) are closed. Remaining work (56fs, 5w2z, 8jx5) migrated to ds8.4 and ds8.3. No cycles detected.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q0om","depends_on_id":"bd-zs64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-zs64","title":"[EPIC-INFRA] Bead Harmonization - API & Agent Interface Coherence","description":"# [EPIC-INFRA] Bead Harmonization - API & Agent Interface Coherence

## Purpose
Master epic tracking bead harmonization: merge duplicates, standardize naming, fix priorities, migrate orphan sub-tasks. NO FEATURE LOSS.

## Sub-Tasks
1. bd-q0om: Merge I/O EPICs (90l9 → ds8)
2. bd-hzrb: Merge Parallel Runtime EPICs (n5o → xrc)
3. bd-kh65: Standardize EPIC naming convention
4. bd-4rpn: Fix priority alignments
5. bd-3p6e: Migrate orphan sub-tasks to hierarchical IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All duplicate EPICs merged or closed with migration notes
- [ ] All EPICs follow [EPIC-{category}] naming convention
- [ ] Priority follows dependency order (parents ≤ children)
- [ ] All sub-tasks use hierarchical IDs ({epic}.{n}.{m})
- [ ] No cycles in dependency graph (verified via `bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'`)
- [ ] `bv --robot-triage` returns healthy project metrics

## Verification Commands
```bash
bv --robot-triage | jq '.quick_ref'
bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'
br list --status=closed | grep "DUPLICATE\|MIGRATED"
```

## Logging Strategy
- All changes logged in docs/bead-harmonization-migration.md
- Each bead update includes reason in description
- Git commit messages reference bead IDs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:27:46.833876766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:50.988353887Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:13.406855498Z","close_reason":"All harmonization sub-tasks closed; naming/priority alignment verified; orphans migrated; no dependency cycles","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-zs64","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Checked sub-tasks: bd-q0om/bd-hzrb/bd-kh65/bd-4rpn/bd-3p6e all closed. Verified xrc/n5o hierarchy, epic naming consistent, priorities aligned (per bd-4rpn close_reason), and br dep cycles empty.","created_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:07Z"},{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-zs64","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ran bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles' => null (no cycles). Orphan migration (bd-3p6e) verified and logged in docs/bead-harmonization-migration.md.","created_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:50Z"}]}
